## How It's Calculated

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a specialized professional. Values are updated to 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: What Ethernet cable do I need for home use?
faq.a: Cat 6 is sufficient for up to 1 Gbps at 100m, which covers most residential needs.
Cat 6a and above support 10 Gbps up to 100m. Cat 6 only reaches 37m at 10 Gbps speeds.

faq.q: Should I use shielded Ethernet cables?
faq.a: Shielded twisted pair (STP) cables are recommended in environments with electromagnetic interference.
Consider fiber optic for distances beyond 100m or when you need very high speeds.

faq.q: Do all cables support PoE (Power over Ethernet)?
faq.a: Yes, all categories support PoE. For high wattage applications, Cat 6 and above is recommended.
